title: Song of the Year Grammy Odds and Predictions - Action Network
text:
Song of the Year Grammy Odds and Predictions

Overview: The 68th Annual Grammy Awards took place on February 1, 2026, at Crypto.com Arena in Los Angeles. Polymarket allowed users to wager on which song would win Song of the Year.

Top Contenders:

"Golden" (KPop Demon Hunters) - Leading the Polymarket board is this track from the Netflix film, performed by Ejae, Audrey Nuna, and Rei Ami. The song "reached No. 1 on the Billboard Global 200," with international success driven by the movie's popularity.

"Luther" (Kendrick Lamar feat. SZA) - The main challenger comes from Kendrick Lamar, who won last year's Song of the Year award and aims to repeat with this collaboration.

"DTMF" (Bad Bunny) - This track showcases "stylistic versatility — blending rap, Latin pop, and reggaeton" and titles his sixth solo album.

Other Notable Nominees:
- "Abracadabra" by Lady Gaga
- "APT" by Rosé and Bruno Mars
- "Wildflower" by Billie Eilsch
- "Manchild" by Sabrina Carpenter
- "Anxiety" by Doechii

The market reflects K-pop's "growing influence across the globe" alongside established artists competing for the prestigious honor.
